title: Contributing to these docs
description: How to add factory documentation pages to the you-agent-factory docs site without recreating retired product patterns.
openingSummary: This page is the published contributing surface for the you-agent-factory docs site. Use it when you want to add factory documentation without reverse-engineering the repository layout.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: Contributing to these docs explains how to add pages to the you-agent-factory documentation site. The site documents the you-agent-factory CLI and agent-factory workflow system: install and first run, use-case guides, concepts, techniques, documentation topics, glossary entries, and blog posts. It is not a benchmark leaderboard and not a paper-download mirror.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: Published collections live under guides, concepts, techniques, documentation, and glossary, plus narrative posts under blog. Each canonical docs page keeps structure in MDX, reader-facing prose in colocated message files, and discovery metadata in a registry record. Choose the collection that matches the reader job, then follow the matching production template.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Pick a collection, copy the matching production template and starter message and asset sidecars, fill isolation-first prose in messages, and add the matching registry record. Run the ordinary local validation path before opening a pull request. Deeper maintainer runbooks stay in the repository contributing guide and site fundamentals; this page is the published orientation, not a full paste of those runbooks.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is the published contributing orientation for factory docs. It is not the full maintainer runbook, not a paste of every validation command, and not a place to recreate retired collection ownership. Product framing for the CLI itself belongs on what is you-agent-factory; first-run steps belong on getting started.
